Name: Adrienne Kowalski
Education: B.A. Elementary Education, University of Michigan, 2014. M.Ed. Educational Leadership, University of Michigan, 2016. Graduated with distinction; capstone project examined how structured morning meeting routines affect classroom community and early literacy outcomes in grades two and three.
Work Experience: Second and third grade classroom teacher, Riverside Elementary School (2014 - 2018): developed a schoolwide read-aloud calendar adopted by all primary grades and led a voluntary after-school tutoring group each semester. Assistant Principal, Riverside Elementary School (2018 - 2022): managed daily building operations, coordinated family engagement events, and supervised a staff of thirty-four teachers and paraprofessionals. Acting Principal, Riverside Elementary School (2022 - present): assumed full leadership responsibilities following the retirement of the previous principal; oversaw two consecutive accreditation reviews with no findings requiring corrective action.
Notes: Strong communication skills noted by the full panel. Expressed genuine interest in mentoring early-career teachers. Salary expectation is slightly above the posted band but may be negotiable given her depth of site-level leadership experience.

Name: Marcus Delgado
Education: B.A. Child Development, University of Texas at Austin, 2011.
Work Experience: Fourth and fifth grade teacher, Cedarwood Elementary School (2011 - 2017): consistently received strong classroom observation ratings and selected by grade-level colleagues to facilitate professional development workshops on differentiated instruction. Curriculum Coordinator, Cedarwood Elementary School (2017 - 2021): aligned the school’s language arts and mathematics scope and sequence documents to updated state standards, working closely with grade-level teams over two full academic years. Instructional Coach, Westfield Elementary School (2021 - present): provides weekly coaching cycles to classroom teachers, conducts demonstration lessons, and leads collaborative planning sessions for all grade bands.

Name: Priya Nambiar
Education: B.Ed. Primary Education, University of Delhi, 2013. M.Ed. Curriculum and Instruction, Georgia State University, 2015. Currently enrolled in a doctoral program in Educational Leadership at Georgia State University; completed all coursework and comprehensive examinations and is in the dissertation writing stage. Withdrew temporarily from the program in 2017 to accept a full-time school leadership role but re-enrolled in 2021.
Notes: Referred by current assistant principal Tomas Ferreira. Panel flagged a potential concern about expectations around schedule flexibility, as the candidate indicated she would need protected time on certain evenings for dissertation work. Recommend a follow-up conversation to clarify boundaries before advancing.
